---
title: "1st Place at ASAS Midwest 2025 — Lactation Feeding Patterns & Sow Farrowing Efficiency"
description: |
  Honored to receive 1st place in the PhD Oral Competition at the 2025 ASAS Midwest Conference for our research on lactation feeding patterns, litter performance, and sow farrowing efficiency metrics.
author: "Elly Kirwa"
date: "2025-03-10"
categories: [Swine Production, Sow Efficiency, Research, Award]
sidebar: false
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

Honored and grateful — our presentation at the **2025 American Society of Animal Science (ASAS) Midwest Conference** was awarded **1st place in the PhD Oral Competition!**

## The Research

The talk explored the relationship between **lactation feeding patterns, litter performance, and sow characteristics** on sow farrowing efficiency metrics — underscoring the significance of data-driven solutions in swine production.

**Key question:** Can we use what happens during lactation to predict whether a sow will successfully farrow in her next cycle?

Our analysis shows that lactation feed intake patterns, combined with litter size and sow parity, provide meaningful predictive signals. By integrating these factors into a decision-support framework, producers can identify sows at elevated risk of farrowing failure before the next breeding cycle — enabling targeted interventions that protect herd productivity and profitability.

## Why This Matters

Every farrowing failure represents a direct economic loss — in feed, labor, and lost piglets. Data-driven early warning tools give producers the chance to act before losses accumulate.

## Acknowledgements

A huge thank you to my advisor **Dr. Gustavo Silva**, collaborators, and the incredible **FieldEpi team** at Iowa State University for the valuable discussions and support throughout this work.
